{"id":"chatterbox-e0z","title":"Rename chatterbox-server to chatterbox with serve/start/stop subcommands","description":"Refactor the CLI entrypoint to rename the 'chatterbox-server' binary to 'chatterbox' (update pyproject.toml [project.scripts] entry). Restructure the CLI with subcommands:

- serve: runs chatterbox in foreground (current default behavior). Logs to stderr unless --log-file is given.
- start: starts chatterbox as a background daemon. Stores logs at ./logs/chatterbox.log (relative to cwd), overridable with --log-file. Stores pidfile at ./logs/chatterbox.pid (relative to cwd), overridable with --pid-file. Creates the logs/ directory if it does not exist.
- stop: reads the pidfile (default ./logs/chatterbox.pid, overridable with --pid-file) and sends SIGTERM to stop the background service.

All existing arguments (--mode, --rest, --rest-port, --whisper-model, etc.) should be available under 'serve' and 'start'. The 'stop' subcommand only needs --pid-file.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:29Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:29Z"}

{"id":"chatterbox-e0z","title":"Rename chatterbox-server to chatterbox with serve/start/stop subcommands","description":"Refactor the CLI entrypoint to rename the 'chatterbox-server' binary to 'chatterbox' (update pyproject.toml [project.scripts] entry). Restructure the CLI with subcommands:

- serve: runs chatterbox in foreground (current default behavior). Logs to stderr unless --log-file is given.
- start: starts chatterbox as a background daemon. Stores logs at ./logs/chatterbox.log (relative to cwd), overridable with --log-file. Stores pidfile at ./logs/chatterbox.pid (relative to cwd), overridable with --pid-file. Creates the logs/ directory if it does not exist.
- stop: reads the pidfile (default ./logs/chatterbox.pid, overridable with --pid-file) and sends SIGTERM to stop the background service.

All existing arguments (--mode, --rest, --rest-port, --whisper-model, etc.) should be available under 'serve' and 'start'. The 'stop' subcommand only needs --pid-file.","status":"ready","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:29Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:52Z"}

{"id":"chatterbox-pjv","title":"Add connection logging and --verbose mode","description":"Improve logging in the chatterbox server:

1. Non-verbose (always on): Log whenever a new Wyoming connection is made (client address, timestamp). This is important for diagnosing Home Assistant connectivity.

2. --verbose flag (available on both 'serve' and 'start' subcommands): Enables detailed logging for:
   - Wyoming protocol: log each inbound/outbound Wyoming message (type, size, direction)
   - STT/TTS services: log Whisper call details (audio duration, processing time, transcript result) and Piper call details (text input, synthesis time, output size)
   - Ollama/LLM interactions: log the prompt sent to the LLM and the response received (with model name and timing)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:36Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:36Z"}

{"id":"chatterbox-slj","title":"Test all ha-emulator features, fix issues, and commit","description":"Run the full end-to-end test workflow and fix any problems discovered:

1. Start server: 'chatterbox start', then 'tail logs/chatterbox.log' to verify clean startup.
2. Run all 4 ha-emulator subcommands:
   - ha-emulator stt (STT-only suite against a test corpus)
   - ha-emulator tts (TTS synthesis test)
   - ha-emulator full (full STT+TTS round-trip suite)
   - ha-emulator single-stt (single-file transcription test)
3. Fix any problems discovered during ha-emulator testing.
4. Run the full pytest suite (pytest -v, excluding integration tests that require live external services).
5. Fix any test failures.
6. 'chatterbox stop' to cleanly shut down the background server.
7. Commit all changes when both ha-emulator tests and pytests pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:43Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T17:52:43Z"}

new_value: All ha-emulator tests passing (14/16 STT, 16/16 TTS, 14/16 full). All 358 pytest tests passing. STT service now properly initialized in full mode with audio resampling support.

{"id":"chatterbox-cxg","title":"Add mellona as a dependency of chatterbox","description":"Add mellona as an explicit dependency in chatterbox's pyproject.toml. This is the foundational prerequisite for all mellona migration tasks.

Steps:
1. Add mellona to chatterbox/pyproject.toml dependencies:
   [project]
   dependencies = [
     ...existing deps...
     'mellona>=0.1.0',  # or the appropriate version specifier
   ]

2. If mellona is not yet on PyPI (it's a submodule), configure it as a local path dependency:
   [tool.uv.sources]  # or [project.optional-dependencies]
   mellona = { path = '../mellona', editable = true }
   OR in pip terms:
   pip install -e ../mellona

3. Update docs/implementation-reference.md to note mellona dependency

4. Verify mellona is importable from within chatterbox:
   python -c 'import mellona; print(mellona.__version__)'

5. Ensure mellona installs correctly in chatterbox's virtual environment

Note: mellona requires aiohttp (not declared in its pyproject.toml) -- also pip install aiohttp.

This is blocked by mellona having a working STT/TTS implementation (hentown mellona issues must be complete first).
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T18:39:58Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T18:39:58Z"}

{"id":"chatterbox-e8y","title":"Create mellona config for chatterbox integration","description":"Create a chatterbox-specific mellona config file that defines the STT, TTS, and LLM providers chatterbox will use. This config serves as the bridge between chatterbox's settings system and mellona's provider system.

Steps:
1. Create config file at chatterbox's config location (e.g., src/chatterbox/mellona.yaml or read from chatterbox Settings):
   providers:
     faster_whisper:
       model: ${CHATTERBOX_STT_MODEL}   # default: base
       device: ${CHATTERBOX_STT_DEVICE}  # default: cpu
     piper:
       voice: ${CHATTERBOX_TTS_VOICE}    # default: en_US-lessac-medium
     ollama:
       base_url: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_BASE_URL}  # default: http://localhost:11434/v1

   stt_profiles:
     default:
       provider: faster_whisper

   tts_profiles:
     default:
       provider: piper

   profiles:
     default:
       provider: ollama
       model: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_MODEL}  # default: llama3.1:8b
       temperature: ${CHATTERBOX_OLLAMA_TEMPERATURE}  # default: 0.7

2. Update chatterbox Settings class to include a mellona_config_path setting
3. Determine loading strategy: should chatterbox pass config_chain to MellonaConfig, or use mellona's default discovery?
4. Document the config approach in dev_notes/

This issue depends on: chatterbox-cxg (mellona installed)

{"id":"chatterbox-44u","title":"Update VoiceAssistantAgent to use mellona config for Ollama","description":"Update VoiceAssistantAgent (src/chatterbox/agent.py) to read its Ollama LLM configuration from mellona's config system rather than directly from chatterbox's Settings. Keep LangChain as the agent orchestration framework; mellona just provides the config values.

Current implementation:
  self.llm = ChatOpenAI(
      base_url=ollama_base_url,  # from chatterbox Settings
      api_key='ollama',
      model=ollama_model,
      temperature=ollama_temperature,
  )

New implementation:
  from mellona import MellonaConfig
  # Load mellona config using chatterbox's configured mellona config path
  mellona_config = MellonaConfig(...)
  profile = mellona_config.get_profile('default')  # or chatterbox's configured profile
  
  self.llm = ChatOpenAI(
      base_url=profile.metadata.get('base_url', 'http://localhost:11434/v1'),
      api_key='ollama',
      model=profile.model,
      temperature=profile.temperature,
  )

Scope of change:
- src/chatterbox/agent.py: Read LLM config from mellona profile instead of Settings
- src/chatterbox/config/__init__.py: Add mellona_profile setting (which mellona LLM profile to use)
- Do NOT replace LangChain - keep ChatOpenAI, ConversationBufferWindowMemory, agent executor
- Update tests that mock LLM initialization

Benefits: Ollama URL, model, and temperature now configurable via mellona config system. Consistent config source across all LLM usage in the stack.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)

{"id":"chatterbox-f1p","title":"Replace WhisperSTTService with mellona STT in chatterbox server","description":"Replace chatterbox's direct faster-whisper usage (WhisperSTTService) with mellona's STT provider in the core server components. The STT interface should remain async; mellona handles the underlying provider.

Files to modify:
- src/chatterbox/services/stt.py: Replace WhisperSTTService implementation to use mellona's transcribe() instead of faster-whisper directly. Keep the same async interface so callers don't need to change.
  OR: Remove stt.py entirely and update all callers to use mellona directly.
- src/chatterbox/adapters/wyoming/server.py: Update STT call sites if WhisperSTTService is removed
- src/chatterbox/adapters/rest/api.py: Update /stt and /stt/file endpoints to use mellona
- src/chatterbox/main.py: Remove STT-specific CLI args (--whisper-model, --whisper-cache-dir) that are now handled by mellona config

Migration pattern:
  # OLD: direct faster-whisper usage
  service = WhisperSTTService(model='base', device='cpu')
  result = await service.transcribe(audio_bytes, sample_rate=16000)

  # NEW: mellona STT call
  from mellona import transcribe_audio  # or similar API
  stt_provider = await mellona.get_stt_provider(profile='default')
  result = await stt_provider.transcribe(STTRequest(audio_file_path=tmp_path))

Note: mellona's STT takes a file path, chatterbox works with raw PCM bytes. The migration must handle the conversion (write bytes to temp WAV file, pass path to mellona, clean up).

Remove: faster-whisper from pyproject.toml dependencies (after verifying no other usage)
Update: tests that mock WhisperSTTService

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"james.jurach@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T18:40:21Z","created_by":"James Jurach","updated_at":"2026-02-25T18:40:21Z"}

{"id":"chatterbox-tey","title":"Replace PiperTTSService with mellona TTS in chatterbox server","description":"Replace chatterbox's direct piper-tts usage (PiperTTSService) with mellona's TTS provider in the core server components.

Files to modify:
- src/chatterbox/services/tts.py: Replace PiperTTSService implementation with mellona TTS calls. Keep the same async interface (synthesize() returning bytes) so Wyoming and REST callers don't change.
  OR: Remove tts.py entirely and update all callers to use mellona directly.
- src/chatterbox/adapters/wyoming/server.py: Update TTS call sites (Synthesize event handler)
- src/chatterbox/adapters/rest/api.py: Update /tts endpoint
- src/chatterbox/tools/registry.py: Update TTSTool if it calls PiperTTSService directly
- src/chatterbox/main.py: Remove TTS-specific CLI args (--piper-voice, --piper-cache-dir) that are now mellona config

Migration pattern:
  # OLD: direct piper-tts usage
  service = PiperTTSService(voice='en_US-lessac-medium')
  audio_bytes = await service.synthesize(text)

  # NEW: mellona TTS call
  tts_provider = await mellona.get_tts_provider(profile='default')
  response = await tts_provider.synthesize(TTSRequest(text=text, output_format='wav'))
  audio_bytes = response.audio_data

Audio format note: Chatterbox's Wyoming server expects PCM audio at specific sample rate. Ensure mellona's Piper provider returns compatible format, OR convert after mellona call.

Remove: piper-tts from pyproject.toml dependencies (after verifying no other usage)
Remove: _MockPiperVoice class (no longer needed)
Update: tests that mock PiperTTSService

This issue depends on: chatterbox-e8y (mellona config setup)

{"id":"chatterbox-mja","title":"Validate chatterbox server startup after mellona migration","description":"Verify that the chatterbox server starts correctly after all mellona migration changes, with no import errors or configuration issues.

Test steps:
1. Install updated chatterbox with mellona dependency:
   pip install -e '.[dev]'
   (verify no import errors)

2. Start server in STT+TTS mode:
   chatterbox serve --mode combined --debug --verbose

3. Verify startup log shows:
   - mellona config loaded successfully
   - FasterWhisper model loading (via mellona)
   - Piper TTS model loading (via mellona)
   - No references to old WhisperSTTService or PiperTTSService

4. Start server in full mode (with LLM):
   chatterbox serve --mode full --debug --verbose
   Verify Ollama connection established (via mellona config)

5. Verify REST API health endpoint:
   curl http://localhost:8080/health
   Response should show:
   { 'status': 'healthy', 'stt': true, 'tts': true, 'agent': true }

6. Test STT via REST:
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/stt -F 'file=@tests/fixtures/audio/test.wav'
   Verify transcript returned

7. Test TTS via REST:
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/tts -d '{"text": "Hello world"}'
   Verify audio bytes returned

Use --mellona-config to point to test config file (not personal ~/.config/mellona/config.yaml).

This issue depends on: chatterbox-a4w (deprecated code removed)

{"id":"chatterbox-p1n","title":"Validate ha-emulator end-to-end testing after mellona migration","description":"Run comprehensive ha-emulator tests against the migrated chatterbox server to verify the full voice assistant pipeline works correctly with mellona providing STT and TTS.

Prerequisites: chatterbox server running with mellona migration complete.

Test script: Run ha-emulator against the running server with multiple test inputs.

Test cases (use all WAV fixtures from tests/fixtures/audio/ or chatterbox's existing test corpus):

1. STT-only validation:
   ha-emulator run-stt test.wav
   Verify: transcript matches known text, latency < 3s

2. TTS-only validation:
   ha-emulator run-tts 'Hello, how can I help you today?'
   Verify: valid WAV audio returned, duration reasonable

3. Full round-trip (STT → LLM → TTS):
   ha-emulator run-stt-chat-tts question.wav
   Verify: 
   - Transcript non-empty and sensible
   - LLM response non-empty and relevant
   - TTS audio file is valid WAV with audible content
   - End-to-end latency measured and acceptable

4. Multiple inputs (at least 5 different test WAV files):
   for wav in tests/fixtures/audio/*.wav; do
     ha-emulator run-stt-chat-tts "$wav" --output "/tmp/response_$(basename $wav)"
   done
   Verify all pass without errors

5. Error resilience:
   - Test with invalid/corrupt WAV file
   - Test with very short audio (< 0.5s)
   - Test with silence
   Verify: clean error messages, no server crash

Document: per-test latency, any transcription accuracy issues, any TTS quality observations.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-mja (server validated)

{"id":"chatterbox-w5r","title":"Validate wyoming-tester end-to-end testing after mellona migration","description":"Run wyoming-tester against the migrated chatterbox server to verify the Wyoming protocol pipeline works correctly.

Prerequisites: chatterbox server running with Wyoming protocol enabled.

Test cases:

1. Text-based test (no audio):
   chatterbox-wyoming-client 'What time is it?'
   Verify: response text received and reasonable

2. Audio push-to-talk test:
   wyoming-tester --uri tcp://localhost:10700 --file tests/fixtures/audio/greeting.wav
   Verify: transcription and TTS response received

3. Multiple audio files:
   for wav in tests/fixtures/audio/*.wav; do
     wyoming-tester --uri tcp://localhost:10700 --file "$wav"
   done
   Verify all complete without protocol errors

4. Test different server modes:
   - stt_only mode: send audio, receive transcript
   - tts_only mode: send text, receive audio
   - full mode: send audio, receive audio response

5. Latency validation:
   Measure round-trip time for each test
   Document baseline latency for future regression comparison

6. Protocol compatibility:
   Verify Wyoming protocol messages are correctly formed (no protocol errors in server logs)

Note: wyoming-tester emulates the ESP32-S3-BOX-3B hardware behavior. Correct operation here validates real hardware compatibility.

This issue depends on: chatterbox-mja (server validated)

{"id":"chatterbox-65y","title":"Copy mellona STT/TTS docs into chatterbox project context","description":"Copy or reference the mellona STT/TTS integration documentation into the chatterbox project so that agents working within the chatterbox submodule context have full information about mellona's STT/TTS API without needing to read the mellona source.

Why: Claude Code agents typically work within a single submodule context. By having mellona docs available inside chatterbox, agents can implement mellona calls correctly without leaving the chatterbox directory.

Steps:
1. Copy (or symlink) relevant mellona docs into chatterbox:
   - mellona/docs/stt-tts-integration.md → chatterbox/docs/mellona-stt-tts-integration.md
   - mellona/docs/provider-reference.md (STT/TTS sections) → chatterbox/docs/mellona-provider-reference.md
   - mellona/examples/config.example.yaml (relevant sections) → chatterbox/docs/mellona-config-example.yaml

2. Create chatterbox/docs/mellona-migration-notes.md with:
   - Which mellona version is required
   - How mellona is installed in chatterbox's venv
   - The specific mellona config file chatterbox uses
   - The Python API chatterbox uses (transcribe, synthesize, get_provider)
   - Common patterns specific to chatterbox's needs (PCM bytes → WAV file → mellona → bytes)
   - Any gotchas discovered during migration

3. Update chatterbox/docs/README.md to reference the new mellona docs

4. Mention these docs in AGENTS.md or similar if relevant

This issue depends on: chatterbox-a4w (migration complete, so we know what to document)
